by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD

narrative:
On June 15, 2026, the Karnataka High Court registered an overall hearing rate of 48.9%, taking up 1,192 of the 2,439 scheduled cases. This represents a steady rise in daily throughput compared to the prior week, when the court recorded a 40.1% hearing rate on June 12 (1,099 of 2,741 cases) and a 47.5% rate on June 9. 

Individual roster performance was led by Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Court Hall 19, who managed the day's heaviest docket by hearing 106 of 271 scheduled cases (39.1%). High-efficiency performances were observed in Court Hall 21, where Justice V. Srishananda heard 76 of 95 scheduled cases (80.0%), and Court Hall 26, where Justice S. Rachaiah addressed 45 of 58 cases (77.6%). 

Procedural stages were heavily dominated by Orders, which saw 500 of the 880 scheduled matters successfully heard (56.8%), marking an efficiency gain over the 43.2% conversion rate for Orders recorded on June 12. In contrast, Final Hearings progressed more slowly, converting just 32.0% of 447 scheduled matters. Preliminary Hearings and Admissions achieved conversion rates of 44.0% and 53.8% respectively.

Writ Petitions (WP) constituted the largest case type, with 508 of 1,159 scheduled cases heard (43.8%). This marks an improvement from June 12, when only 30.8% of WPs were heard. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) followed with 170 of 329 cases heard (51.7%). Notable clearance was observed in Regular Second Appeals (RSA), which achieved a 62.7% hearing rate, and Criminal Appeals (CRL.A), which saw 76.6% of its scheduled cases taken up.
